title: Commodity prices declined in June—Pink Sheet
gist: The World Bank's June 2026 Pink Sheet shows broad commodity price declines led by a 17.7% drop in energy (Brent -20.6%), with agriculture, metals, and precious metals all falling and fertilizer plunging 21.8%, while U.S. natural gas bucked the trend up 7.3%.

insights.claim: The World Bank energy price index declined 17.7% in June 2026, largely driven by a 20.6% drop in Brent crude oil prices.
insights.speaker: John Baffes
insights.so_what: A sharp June energy-price decline eases inflation and import-bill pressure for energy importers while squeezing producer revenues.
insights.quote: The energy price index declined 17.7% in June, largely driven by a 20.6% drop in Brent crude oil prices

insights.claim: U.S. natural gas prices rose 7.3% in June 2026, partly offsetting the broader energy-index decline.
insights.speaker: John Baffes
insights.so_what: Natural gas diverging upward from oil signals region-specific supply/demand dynamics worth watching against the global energy selloff.
insights.quote: partly offset by a 7.3% increase in U.S. natural gas

insights.claim: Agricultural prices declined 1.7% in June 2026, with food prices easing 2.6% and fertilizer prices plunging 21.8%, while beverages rose 1.1%.
insights.speaker: John Baffes
insights.so_what: The steep fertilizer drop signals lower input costs for the next crop cycle, potentially supporting farm margins and future food-price disinflation.
insights.quote: food prices easing 2.6% and fertilizer prices plunging 21.8%

insights.claim: Metal prices fell 2.4% in June 2026, led by nickel (-6.5%) and aluminum (-6.2%).
insights.speaker: John Baffes
insights.so_what: Industrial-metal weakness in nickel and aluminum points to softening manufacturing/EV-supply-chain demand signals.
insights.quote: Metal prices fell 2.4% in June, led by declines in nickel (-6.5%) and aluminum (-6.2%)

insights.claim: Precious metals dropped 9.2% in June 2026, driven by declines in silver (-14.6%) and platinum (-13.6%).
insights.speaker: John Baffes
insights.so_what: A double-digit fall in silver and platinum marks a notable reversal in the precious-metals complex, relevant to safe-haven and industrial-demand positioning.
insights.quote: Precious metals dropped 9.2%, driven by declines in silver (-14.6%) and platinum (-13.6%)

insights.claim: The World Bank non-energy commodity index fell 3.2% in June 2026, with raw materials slipping 1.2%.
insights.speaker: John Baffes
insights.so_what: Broad-based non-energy declines reinforce a disinflationary commodity backdrop across the complex heading into mid-2026.
